title: Thymophylla aurea
taxonomy: Thymophylla aurea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the southern regions of Argentina and Chile. This plant belongs to the genus Thymophylla, which consists of several species of daisy-like plants. The exact classification of Thymophylla aurea is Thymophylla aurea, a species within the family Asteraceae and order Asterales. Further classification includes the family being placed in the class Magnoliopsida and phylum Angiospermophyta.
uses: Thymophylla aurea, also known as the golden daisy or yellow daisy, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Thymophylla aurea, also known as Yellow Buttons or Butterweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems.
